ncert_truth_statements: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL): the algebraic sum of currents meeting at any junction in a circuit is zero, based on conservation of charge
The sum of currents flowing into a junction must equal the sum of currents flowing out of that junction at every instant
KCL reflects conservation of charge — electric charge can neither be created nor destroyed at a junction
In steady state, no charge accumulates at any junction in a circuit
KCL applies to any node in a circuit regardless of the types of components connected to it
